name: KrillApp.Trigger.IncomingWebHook
title: Incoming WebHook
description: Registers an HTTP endpoint at /incoming/{path} on the Krill server. When an HTTP request matching the configured path and method is received, all child nodes are executed. This enables external systems to trigger Krill automations via HTTP calls.
shortDescription: Triggers child node execution when an HTTP request is received at a configured endpoint.
llmPurpose: Triggers execution of child nodes when an HTTP request is received at the configured /incoming/{path} endpoint.
llmBehavior: exposes an API endpoint on the server
llmInputs: HTTP request to /incoming/{path} with configured method
llmPromptHints: trigger this from an external API
run this when I send an HTTP request
create an endpoint for this automation
llmCreationHints: Create an IncomingWebHook when the user wants to trigger automation from an external HTTP call. Requires a path string and HTTP method.

llmConnectionHints.role: root trigger node that initiates child execution on incoming HTTP request

llmExamples: Create a webhook endpoint that triggers a data collection.
Set up an API endpoint so I can trigger this from my CI pipeline.
Listen for incoming POST requests to run this automation.
